summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting on February 8, 2022, covering storm response costs, ARP-funded capital projects, a police employee wellness gym, and economic development planning. The board discussed the significant ice storm that cost the township over $15,000, reviewed preliminary pricing for roof replacement, parking lot repaving, and sewer/water line repairs funded by ARP stimulus money, and approved converting a downstairs room into an employee fitness facility. A work session on economic development tools was planned for around March 22nd.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda – passed
Motion to approve payment dates January 26, 2022 through February 8, 2022 – passed
Motion to approve out-of-town travel for Harry Holbert to attend OTA conference in Columbus retroactive to January 27, 2022 at cost not to exceed $300 – passed
Resolution approving major adjustment to PUD ZC-2021-005 at 1592 State Route 28 (The Belmont Phase 2) – passed
Motion to approve Chief Meyers' travel to FDIC in Indianapolis April 26-30, 2022 at cost not to exceed $1,500 – passed
Motion to approve converting a downstairs room into a gym for employee wellness with discussed contingencies – passed
Motion to declare surplus/obsolete items and sell on GovDeals – passed
Motion to approve Sergeant Dylan West becoming a member of Clermont County Special Response Team – passed
Motion to adjourn into executive session at 7:56 PM to discuss appointment, employment, and compensation of a public employee – passed
No motion resulted from executive session; authorization given to begin advertising for a new partially grant-funded police position
Motion to adjourn regular meeting at 8:30 PM – passed
action_items: Administrator Payton to coordinate with Mike Hennekamp on scheduling economic development work session, possibly March 22 or a Saturday
Administrator Payton to bring preliminary pricing on ARP-funded projects (roof, sewer/water lines, parking lot, service garage) to next board meeting
Administrator Payton to re-engage architectural firm regarding service garage project
Chief Rose and Sergeant West to draft restrictive on-duty workout policy and share via email with trustees for review
Chief Rose to obtain pricing for soundproofing, LED lighting, and ceiling improvements for entire downstairs community room area
Chief Rose to begin advertising for new partially grant-funded police department position
Chad (service department) to coordinate delivery of 300 tons of salt to replenish supply
Administrator Payton to explore electronic signage for firehouse and gateway/branding signage using ARP funds
key_amounts: $15,284.94 total storm cost over 2.5 days (not including benefits and retirement)
$9,585 for 145 tons of salt used during storm
1,113 miles driven by 5 trucks during storm
$300 not to exceed for Harry Holbert OTA conference travel
$1,500 not to exceed for Chief Meyers FDIC conference travel
$22,000 maximum total project cost for employee gym
$450,000 for road paving grant match from ARP funds
50% match required on road paving grant
notable_topics: Major ice storm response recap and costs
ARP (American Rescue Plan) fund project planning: roof replacement, sewer/water lines at Station 18, parking lot repaving, service garage, road paving
Economic development work session planning with Mike Hennekamp to discuss TIFs, tax abatements, and other tools
Police department employee wellness gym conversion and on-duty workout policy debate
PUD major adjustment resolution for The Belmont Phase 2 at 1592 State Route 28
Sergeant Dylan West approved as Clermont County SRT negotiator
Executive session regarding creation of partially grant-funded police position
Police officer health statistics and wellness initiatives
attendees: Trustee Haslund (also referred to as Hauserman/Hosmer in transcript)
notes: The transcript contains some speech-to-text errors making some names inconsistent (Haslund/Hauserman/Hosmer appear to be the same trustee). The meeting included recognition of the passing of Bruce Tartar, a CPA member and community volunteer, and the loss of Donna Mozik's niece. The executive session discussed a new partially grant-funded police position but no formal decision was made. The board expressed strong support for the wellness gym but had concerns about the on-duty workout policy, requesting email review of the draft policy before finalizing.
title: BOT 2.8.22.mp4
